author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
The First Man Himself Also Might Have Stood by His Free Will.
title: The First Man Himself Also Might Have Stood by His Free Will.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Thus also He made man with free will; and although ignorant of his future fall, yet therefore happy, because he thought it was in his own power both not to die and not to become miserable.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And if he had willed by his own free will to continue in this state of uprightness and freedom from sin, assuredly without any experience of death and of unhappiness he would have received by the merit of that continuance the fulness of blessing with which the holy angels also are blessed; that is, the impossibility of falling any more, and the knowledge of this with absolute certainty.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: For even he himself could not be blessed although in Paradise, nay, he would not be there, where it would not become him to be miserable, if the foreknowledge of his fall had made him wretched with the dread of such a disaster.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: But because he forsook God of his free will, he experienced the just judgment of God, that with his whole race, which being as yet all placed in him had sinned with him, he should be condemned.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: For as mary of this race as are delivered by God’s grace are certainly delivered from the condemnation in which they are already held bound.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Whence, even if none should be delivered, no one could justly blame the judgment of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: That, therefore, in comparison of those that perish few, but in their absolute number many, are delivered, is effected by grace, is effected freely: thanks must be given, because it is effected, so that no one may be lifted up as of his own deservings, but that every mouth may be stopped, and he that glorieth may glory in the Lord.
